qa_pairs.question: How can I check the size of a file, directory, or archive from the terminal?
qa_pairs.answer: You can use the command `du -sh [your_file ...]` from your terminal to see the size of a file, directory, or archive.

qa_pairs.question: Could you provide an example of checking the size of multiple files or directories using `du -sh`?
qa_pairs.answer: Certainly. An example command is `du -sh results.tar work tests`, which might output `112K results.tar`, `58K work`, and `48K tests`.

qa_pairs.question: What utility can I use to divide large files or tar files into smaller parts?
qa_pairs.answer: You can use the `split` command to divide large files or tar files into smaller parts.

qa_pairs.question: What is the syntax for splitting a file using the `split` command?
qa_pairs.answer: The syntax for splitting a file is `split -b <Size-in-MB><file or tar-file-name><prefix-name>`.

qa_pairs.question: What does the `-b` option do in the `split` command?
qa_pairs.answer: The `-b` option in the `split` command is invoked to fix the size of the small parts.

qa_pairs.question: If I split a file named `results.tar` into 100MB parts with the prefix `small-res`, how will the smaller files be named?
qa_pairs.answer: The small files will be named starting from `small-resaa`, `small-resab`, `small-resac`, `small-resad`, and so on, in your current working directory.

qa_pairs.question: How do I recover the original file after it has been split into smaller parts using the `split` command?
qa_pairs.answer: To recover the original file, you use the `cat` command as follows: `cat small_res* > your_archive_name.tar`.

qa_pairs.question: What option can I use with the `split` command if I want to append numbers instead of alphabets to the split file names?
qa_pairs.answer: You can use the `-d` option in the `split` command to append numbers in place of alphabets.

qa_pairs.question: What does the `pwd` command do?
qa_pairs.answer: The `pwd` (present work directory) command shows you the current working path.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the `ls` command?
qa_pairs.answer: The `ls` (list) command is used to see the files and sub-directories.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to have free space when using `gzip` or `bzip2` commands?
qa_pairs.answer: Applying `gzip` or `bzip2` to a file requires free space to create the final compressed file. These commands will fail if there is no space left on the device or if you are out of quota.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check if I have enough disk space or quota on CC clusters before compressing files?
qa_pairs.answer: On CC clusters, you can use the command `quota` or `quota -s` from your terminal to see if you have enough space in a more human-readable format.
